title: Sannyasi Uprising
provisions: This revolt began around 1770 in Bengal.
It was triggered by British restrictions on the movement of ascetic groups.
The uprising emerged when religious mendicants were prevented from visiting pilgrimage sites.

title: Fakir Uprising
provisions: This revolt began around 1776.
It involved wandering Muslim mendicants.
The rebels formed an alliance with Hindu ascetics against the British.
The novel 'Anand Math' drew inspiration from this uprising.
Chirag Ali, Bhavani Pathak, and Devi Choudharani were associated with this revolt.

title: Pagal Panthi Revolt
provisions: This revolt was linked to a religious sect within Islam.
The movement was founded by a leader named Karam Shah.
Later, his successor mobilized peasants against zamindars.

title: Khasi Uprising
provisions: This tribal uprising took place around 1829.
It opposed British attempts to permanently settle labourers in tribal regions.
The revolt was connected with a British project linking the Brahmaputra Valley and Silhed.
A chief named Tirth Singh provided leadership.
It was associated with unrest involving several hill tribes of Northeast India.

title: Kol Revolt
provisions: This revolt occurred in regions such as Ranchi, Singhbhum, Hazaribagh, and Palamu.
It took place in an area rich in minerals and resources.
The transfer of tribal land to outsiders triggered the uprising.
A leader named Budhu Bhagat organized this rebellion.

title: Paika Rebellion
provisions: This revolt took place in 1817.
It involved a traditional landed militia serving a regional kingdom.
The rebellion was triggered by the revocation of rent-free land under British policies.
Bakshi Jagabandhu led the uprising.

title: Ahom Revolt
provisions: This revolt took place between 1826 and 1828.
It followed British attempts to annex a northeastern kingdom after the First Burma War.
A prince named Gondhar Konwar led the uprising.
The revolt resulted in the restoration of territory to the traditional ruler.

title: Khond Uprising
provisions: This uprising occurred between 1837 and 1857.
It involved tribes inhabiting the Niyamgiri hills.
British attempts to suppress a ritual practice triggered the rebellion.
A leader named Chakra Bisoyi guided the resistance.

title: Santhal Uprising
provisions: This major rebellion took place between 1855 and 1856.
It was one of the most massive tribal uprisings.
The revolt was known as 'Hool' in the local language.
It occurred in the Daman-i-Koh region.
Sidhu and Kanu Murmu led the uprising.

title: Munda Revolt
provisions: This revolt took place in 1899.
Illegal transfer of tribal land to outsiders triggered the uprising.
Birsa Munda provided leadership.
The leader initially pursued legal remedies before launching the rebellion.
He was arrested, tried, and died in captivity.

title: Wahabi Movement
provisions: This movement was a religious revivalist reform movement within Islam.
It was inspired by the ideology of Abdul Wahab.
Shah Waliullah of Delhi propagated similar ideas in India.
Syed Ahmad of Rai Bareilly was associated with this movement.
Followers participated in the Revolt of 1857.

title: Kuka Movement
provisions: This movement was a religious reform movement within Sikhism.
It was initiated by Bhagat Jawahar Mal.
After the annexation of Punjab, it turned against British rule.
Followers participated in the Revolt of 1857.

title: Bhil Uprising
provisions: This uprising took place between 1822 and 1846.
It involved tribal communities of the Western Ghats.
The revolt opposed British forest policies.
A leader named Sevram provided leadership.
The British later allowed use of forest produce to pacify the rebels.

title: Kohli Uprising
provisions: This revolt involved a fishing community of the Western Ghats.
The destruction of their forts by the British triggered the uprising.

title: Ram-Maosi Revolt
provisions: This revolt involved hill tribes dependent on forests and palm plantations.
British forest restrictions triggered the rebellion.
Chittur Singh led the uprising.
Political disturbances in Satara contributed to this revolt.

title: Gadkari Revolt
provisions: This revolt involved a traditional military class from Maharashtra.
It was triggered by the disbandment of hereditary military units.
The uprising began in Kolhapur.
It later spread to Sawantwadi and Goa.

title: Revolt of Raja of Vizianagaram
provisions: This revolt took place in 1794.
It followed heavy monetary demands imposed by the British.
The Battle of Padmabhim was associated with this conflict.
The ruler was ultimately defeated.

title: Rampa Rebellion
provisions: This revolt took place in 1879.
It involved tribal communities of the Eastern Ghats.
British forest regulations triggered the uprising.

title: Revolt of Dewan Velu Thampi
provisions: This revolt took place in 1805.
It was led by the Diwan of Travancore.
The uprising occurred in a princely state under subsidiary alliance.
